src: ἦν γὰρ περικειμένη Αἴγυπτος τὸν Φαραὼ
tok: pf mp ptcp nom sg f
lit: For Egypt was wrapped around Pharaoh
sm: For Egypt was wrapped around Pharaoh,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the participle περικειμένη further describes Αἴγυπτος.
syn.svo: Subject: Αἴγυπτος. Verb: ἦν. Participial complement: περικειμένη. Object: τὸν Φαραώ.
syn.rhet: Continues the vivid personification of Egypt as a garment or covering.

src: ὡς περιβολὴ κωκυτοῦ.
lit: like a garment of wailing.
sm: like a garment of lamentation.

syn.mc: Comparative nominal phrase dependent on line 131.
syn.svo: No finite verb; phrase elaborates the simile of being wrapped.
syn.rhet: The garment metaphor is sharpened by the genitive of lamentation.

src: τοιοῦτος ὑφάνθη κιθὼν τῷ τυραννικῷ σώματι:
ana: τοιοῦτος ὑφάνθη χιτὼν τῷ τυραννικῷ σώματι:
lit: Such a tunic was woven for the tyrannical body:
sm: Such a tunic was woven for the tyrannical body:

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the dative marks the body for which the tunic is woven.
syn.svo: Subject: χιτών. Verb: ὑφάνθη. Dative of advantage: τῷ τυραννικῷ σώματι.
syn.rhet: The metaphor of clothing is carried forward from the previous line.

src: τοιαύτην ἐνέδυσεν στολὴν τὸν σκληρὸν Φαραὼ
lit: He clothed stubborn Pharaoh with such a garment
sm: He clothed stubborn Pharaoh with such a garment,

syn.sub: The subject is completed by line 135.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐνέδυσεν. Direct object: στολήν. Secondary object: τὸν σκληρὸν Φαραώ. Subject appears in the next line.
syn.rhet: The shame-clothing of Pharaoh is stated before the agent is named.

src: ὁ τῆς δικαιοσύνης ἄγγελος"
lit: the angel of justice.
sm: the angel of justice.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.svo: Subject phrase: ὁ τῆς δικαιοσύνης ἄγγελος.
syn.app: The genitive phrase τῆς δικαιοσύνης qualifies ἄγγελος.
syn.rhet: The delayed subject heightens the force of the clothing metaphor.

src: πένθος πικρὸν καὶ σκότος ψηλαφητόν,
lit: bitter mourning and palpable darkness,
sm: bitter mourning and palpable darkness,

syn.mc: Continuation of the object phrase governed by ἐνέδυσεν in line 134.
syn.svo: Accusative nouns and adjectives continue the content of the garment.
syn.rhet: The robe is described by a piling up of horror nouns.

src: καὶ ἀτεκνίαν καινὴν ἐπὶ τῶν πρωτοτόκων αὐτῆς.
lit: and new childlessness over her firstborn.
sm: and new childlessness over her firstborn.

syn.mc: Continuation of the accusative object phrase from line 134.
syn.svo: Object continuation: ἀτεκνίαν καινήν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῶν πρωτοτόκων αὐτῆς.
syn.rhet: The garment imagery now names the plague's result directly.

src: ἣν γὰρ ταχινὸς καὶ ἀκόρεστος ὁ τῶν πρωτοτόκων θάνατος,
ana: ἦν γὰρ ταχινὸς καὶ ἀκόρεστος ὁ τῶν πρωτοτόκων θάνατος,
lit: For swift and insatiable was the death of the firstborn,
sm: For swift and insatiable was the death of the firstborn,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; γάρ explains the preceding description.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ θάνατος. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjectives: ταχινός, ἀκόρεστος. Dependent genitive: τῶν πρωτοτόκων.
syn.rhet: A gnomic-style statement frames the plague's speed and appetite.

src: ἦν [δὲ] καινὸν τρόπαιον ἰδεῖν
lit: But it was a new trophy to see
sm: But it was a new trophy to see,

syn.mc: Impersonal/verbless-style main clause with infinitival complement.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the infinitive ἰδεῖν supplies the viewing perspective.
syn.svo: Verb: ἦν. Predicate complement: καινὸν τρόπαιον. Complementary infinitive: ἰδεῖν.
syn.rhet: The syntax turns from description to spectacle.

src: ἐπὶ τῶν πιπτόντων νεκρῶν ἐν μιᾷ ῥοπῇ.
tok: pres act ptcp gen pl m
lit: upon the falling dead in a single moment.
sm: upon the falling dead in a single moment.

syn.svo: The phrase localizes the spectacle described in line 139.
syn.ptc: πιπτόντων is attributive, describing the νεκροί.
syn.rhet: A compressed visual snapshot of simultaneous collapse.

src: καὶ ἐγένετο τοῦ θανάτου τροφὴ
lit: And it became food of death
sm: And it became food for death,

syn.mc: Main clause with delayed subject in the next line.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: τροφή τοῦ θανάτου. Subject is supplied by line 142.
syn.rhet: The horrific image turns the dead into nourishment for death.

src: ἡ τῶν κειμένων τροπή.
tok: pres mp ptcp gen pl m
lit: the turning of the fallen.
sm: the collapse of those lying fallen.

syn.mc: Completes the clause begun in line 141.
syn.svo: Subject of ἐγένετο: ἡ τῶν κειμένων τροπή. Predicate nominative: τροφή τοῦ θανάτου.
syn.ptc: κειμένων is substantival, modifying the fallen dead.
syn.rhet: The finality of death is phrased as a turning, a collapse.

src: καινὴν δὲ συμφορὰν ἐὰν ἀκούσητε θαυμάσετε'
ana: καινὴν δὲ συμφορὰν ἐὰν ἀκούσητε θαυμάσετε.
lit: But if you hear of a new calamity, you will marvel.
sm: But if you hear of a new calamity, you will marvel.
emd: Trailing OCR apostrophe normalized to a period.

syn.sub: ἐὰν ἀκούσητε is the protasis; θαυμάσετε is the apodosis.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: θαυμάσετε. Protasis: ἐὰν ἀκούσητε. Object of hearing: καινὴν συμφοράν.
syn.rhet: The speaker calls for wonder before narrating the details.

src: τάδε γὰρ περιέσχεν τοὺς Αἰγυπτίους,
lit: For these things encompassed the Egyptians,
sm: For these things encompassed the Egyptians,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the following lines enumerate τάδε.
syn.svo: Subject: τάδε. Verb: περιέσχεν. Object: τοὺς Αἰγυπτίους.
syn.rhet: Demonstrative τάδε introduces the ensuing catalog.

src: νὺξ μακρὰ
lit: a long night
sm: a long night

syn.mc: Nominal appositive or explanatory fragment to line 145.
syn.svo: No verb; the phrase elaborates τάδε.
syn.rhet: First item in a rapid explanatory list.

src: καὶ σκότος ψηλαφητὸν
lit: and palpable darkness
sm: and palpable darkness

syn.svo: No verb; continues the list of the dark conditions.
syn.rhet: The list gains tactile force.

src: καὶ θάνατος ψηλαφῶν
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and death groping
sm: and death groping

syn.mc: Nominal/participial fragment in the explanatory list.
syn.svo: Death is personified with a present participle.
syn.rhet: Personification intensifies the menace.

src: καὶ ἄγγελος ἐκθλίβων
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and an angel oppressing
sm: and an angel oppressing

syn.mc: Nominal/participial fragment in the explanatory list.
syn.svo: Angel personified by a present participle.
syn.ptc: ἐκθλίβων is attributive, describing the angel as pressing or crushing.
syn.rhet: The list sharpens from darkness to active crushing power.

src: καὶ ἅδης καταπίνων τοὺς πρωτοτόκους αὐτῶν.
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and Hades swallowing up their firstborn.
sm: and Hades swallowing up their firstborn.

syn.mc: Final member of the explanatory list begun in line 145.
syn.svo: Subject: ἅδης. Participle: καταπίνων. Object: τοὺς πρωτοτόκους αὐτῶν.
syn.rhet: The list reaches its climax in Hades devouring the firstborn.

src: τὸ δὲ καινότερον καὶ φοβερώτερον ἀκοῦσαι ἔχετε
lit: But you have the newer and more fearful thing to hear
sm: But you have the newer and more fearful thing to hear,

syn.sub: No subordinate finite clause; ἀκοῦσαι complements ἔχετε.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: ἔχετε. Complement: τὸ καινότερον καὶ φοβερώτερον ἀκοῦσαι.
syn.rhet: The speaker pivots from summary to deeper wonder.

src: ἐν τῷ ψηλαφητῷ σκότει ὁ ἀψηλάφητος θάνατος ἐκρύβετο,
lit: In the palpable darkness the untouchable death was hidden,
sm: In the palpable darkness the untouchable death was hidden,

syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἀψηλάφητος θάνατος. Verb: ἐκρύβετο. Locative phrase: ἐν τῷ ψηλαφητῷ σκότει.
syn.rhet: Paradox: palpable darkness hides untouchable death.

src: καὶ τὸ μὲν σκότος ἐφηλάφων οἱ δυστυχεῖς Αἰγύπτιοι,
lit: And the unfortunate Egyptians were feeling the darkness on the one hand,
sm: And the unfortunate Egyptians were feeling the darkness,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; μὲν anticipates the contrast with line 154.
syn.svo: Subject: οἱ δυστυχεῖς Αἰγύπτιοι. Verb: ἐφηλάφων. Object: τὸ σκότος.
syn.rhet: Antithesis begins: Egyptians grope at darkness, death gropes at firstborn.

src: ὁ δὲ θάνατος ἐξεραυνῶν ἐψηλάφα τοὺς πρωτοτόκους τῶν Aἰγυπτίων
ana: ὁ δὲ θάνατος ἐξεραυνῶν ἐψηλάφα τοὺς πρωτοτόκους τῶν Αἰγυπτίων
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: But death, searching thoroughly, was feeling about the firstborn of the Egyptians
sm: But death, searching thoroughly, was feeling about the firstborn of the Egyptians,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: Latin A -> Greek Α in Αἰγυπτίων.

syn.svo: Subject: ὁ θάνατος. Verb: ἐψηλάφα. Object: τοὺς πρωτοτόκους τῶν Αἰγυπτίων. Participle: ἐξεραυνῶν.
syn.ptc: ἐξεραυνῶν is circumstantial, intensifying the groping image.
syn.rhet: The antithesis to line 153 is complete: death gropes for the firstborn.

src: τοῦ ἀγγέλου κελεύοντος.
tok: pres act ptcp gen sg m
lit: while the angel was commanding.
sm: while the angel was commanding.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause beyond the absolute construction.
syn.svo: Genitive absolute with explicit subject τοῦ ἀγγέλου and participle κελεύοντος.
syn.ptc: κελεύοντος forms a genitive absolute with τοῦ ἀγγέλου.
syn.rhet: The command remains active even while the consequences unfold.

src: εἴ τις οὖν ἐφηλάφα τὸ σκότος
lit: If then anyone was feeling the darkness
sm: If, then, anyone was feeling the darkness,

syn.sub: εἴ ... ἐφηλάφα is the protasis; line 157 is the apodosis.
syn.svo: Subject: τις. Verb: ἐφηλάφα. Object: τὸ σκότος.
syn.rhet: The general rule is stated after the specific Egyptian scene.

src: ὑπὸ τοῦ θανάτου ἐξήγετο.
lit: he was led out by death.
sm: he was led out by death.

syn.svo: Subject: implied τις. Verb: ἐξήγετο. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τοῦ θανάτου.
syn.rhet: The condition is fatal: whoever groped darkness was seized by death.

src: καί τις πρωτότοκος χειρὶ σκοτεινὸν σῶμα ἐναγκαλισάμενος
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: And a certain firstborn, having embraced a dark body with the hand,
sm: And a certain firstborn, having embraced a dark body with his hand,
emd: <UNCERTAIN: syntactic force of χειρὶ σκοτεινὸν σῶμα ἐναγκαλισάμενος is unclear>

syn.mc: Participial/conditional fragment continuing the hypothetical sequence.
syn.sub: No finite clause in this line; the construction continues into line 159.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τις πρωτότοκος. Participle: ἐναγκαλισάμενος. Object phrase: σκοτεινὸν σῶμα. Dative χειρὶ is instrumental or otherwise uncertain.
syn.ptc: The participial phrase is syntactically compressed and somewhat uncertain.
syn.rhet: The scene remains vivid but grammatically compressed.

src: τῇ ψυχῇ ἐκδειματωθεὶς οἰκτρὸν καὶ φοβερὸν ἀνεβόησεν"
ana: τῇ ψυχῇ ἐκδειματωθεὶς οἰκτρὸν καὶ φοβερὸν ἀνεβόησεν·
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: Terrified in soul, he cried out pitifully and fearfully.
sm: Terrified in soul, he cried out pitifully and fearfully.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to raised-dot punctuation introducing the cry in line 160.

syn.mc: Main clause completing the hypothetical scene begun in line 158.
syn.svo: Subject: implied τις πρωτότοκος. Verb: ἀνεβόησεν. Dative of respect: τῇ ψυχῇ. Participle: ἐκδειματωθείς.
syn.ptc: ἐκδειματωθείς is circumstantial, with τῇ ψυχῇ as dative of respect.
syn.rhet: The fear becomes audible in direct lament.

src: Τίνα κρατεῖ ἡ δεξιά μου;
lit: Whom does my right hand hold?
sm: Whom does my right hand hold?

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the question continues into the following line(s).
syn.svo: Interrogative object: Τίνα. Verb: κρατεῖ. Subject: ἡ δεξιά μου.
syn.rhet: The firstborn's speech turns from description into direct questioning.
